title: The First EA Job
content: My first EA job? I was twenty-two, fresh out of college, working for a CEO who got four hundred emails a day. First week, I missed one. ONE. It was from a board member. I've never missed another one since.
extended_content: Gloria Martinez was tough. High standards. She didn't yell when I missed that email. She just said: 'Alex, in this job, one is enough.' But she also TAUGHT me. How to prioritize. How to anticipate. How to communicate bad news. How to protect someone's time without being rude. When she was promoted to a bigger company, she asked me to come with her. I said no - I was ready to grow differently. She understood.
when_to_tell: when asked about career origins or defining moments
lesson: In high-stakes communication, every message matters
